description_long: This is a commercial jet airplane with a streamlined fuselage painted primarily white with a red accent stripe running along the length of the body and a red tail fin. The wings are mounted low on the fuselage and are swept back, giving the aircraft a modern, aerodynamic appearance. The airplane features a conventional tail design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The landing gear is visible, with a nose wheel and two main gear units under the wings, suggesting a tricycle configuration. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with a slightly raised canopy and multiple windows. The engines are not externally visible, indicating they may be integrated into the wings or fuselage.
description: A sleek, white and red commercial jet airplane with swept-back low wings, a single vertical tail fin, and a forward cockpit. The landing gear is deployed, and the design is streamlined for flight.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, the red accent stripe, swept-back low wings, and the single vertical tail fin.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the horizontal stabilizers, the single vertical tail fin, and the symmetry of the wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with the landing gear and red accent stripe visible along the fuselage.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the nose, cockpit windows, forward landing gear, and the full wingspan with swept-back design.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with swept-back wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Engines not externally visible; likely integrated within the wings or fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with one nose gear and two main gear units, all deployed.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows at the front of the fuselage.
